sourceShortSummaries: The party descends from the entrance bridge into Zeyfa's Labyrinth and feels its first branching tunnels shift from ordinary stone into something more alive and hostile.
The party studies a warm fissure lined with ice ferns, watches the room grow more violent, and finally commits to crossing after Eolo's failed leap proves how dangerous it is.
The party forces a crossing over the fissure and pushes onward through storm-lashed corridors where the maze shifts and ice grows beneath every step.
In a frozen corpse chamber, the party questions a dead villager about Melusa and Zeyfa's sacrifices, then loots a few useful relics before moving on.
The party leaves the corpse behind, gets trapped in a looping white maze full of wind-creatures and booming passages, and finally sneaks out through the last remaining exit.
The party breaks into a collapsing ice passage, rides a black chute into a stormless chamber, and ends face to face with the shaggy beast Kalima fears.
sourceLongSummaries: After a short rest and a brief reorientation, the party helps Kalima down from the icy bridge into the labyrinth's entrance chamber, lights the way, and starts testing the first forked passages. The early routes lead through snowy, cramped stone tunnels and dead ends, and Ekko notes that unlike the Great Chasm's abandoned depths, this place feels active and alive as they press deeper toward a warmer chamber ahead.

The tunnels open into a broad chamber split by a dark crack with warm air rising from below and strange ice ferns waving at the edge. As the party experiments with light, distance, and possible routes, the wind and snow intensify around the fissure, Kalima grows more alarmed, and Eolo's slipping attempt shows that the crossing is riskier than it first looked; by the end of the beat, the group decides they have to get everyone across anyway.

Using flight, levitation, rope, and brute force, the party gets everyone across the fissure, with Justas briefly plunging into the gap and discovering a pocket of hot, lightless blackness inside it. Beyond the crossing, the labyrinth becomes harsher rather than safer: the wind howls, exits move, visibility drops, and ice thickens underfoot as the group follows changing passages deeper toward a side chamber ahead.

The party discovers a human corpse pinned to the wall by ice and uses Speak with Dead to learn that he came from Melusa, died when the wind came alive, and had entered the labyrinth as a sunrise sacrifice. Kalima confirms the village name, the corpse describes Melusa as a cliffside settlement fed by a river and fish, and Finnan's ancestral memory links the name to Melua, an old waterfall Kestavo on the Teft; once the questioning ends, the party searches the body and takes a few salvageable items before continuing down the passage.

Past the corpse chamber, the labyrinth turns into a whiteout of snow hubs, booming corridors, and passages that loop back into the same room with fewer exits each time. Kalima is dropped and injured when the kenku try to carry her through the wind, Justas heals her and forces her through on foot, and the party repeatedly dodges the shadowy wind-creatures sweeping the maze until Finnan's ink mark proves the loop and Jrain's Pass Without Trace lets everyone slip through the final opening.

After reaching calmer ground, the party retraces the booming hazard, emerges into a circular snow room, and chooses a right-hand tunnel hung with thin ice veils. Ekko's bonfire shatters the veils and triggers a collapse and avalanche, forcing everyone to jump into darkness and slide down slick black ice into a stormless black-stone chamber where Kalima panics, warns that a beast is here, and hides behind Finnan's illusion just before Justas calls the creature out and a towering shaggy white beast appears with shadowy forms growing nearby.

sourceEntries.title: Into the Labyrinth
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The session opens with the party recovering from the prior fight, choosing an entrance, and probing the first branching tunnels until they reach the warmer chamber split by a dark crack.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party descends from the entrance bridge into Zeyfa's Labyrinth and feels its first branching tunnels shift from ordinary stone into something more alive and hostile.

The early routes lead through snowy, cramped stone tunnels and dead ends, and Ekko notes that unlike the Great Chasm's abandoned depths, this place feels active and alive as they press deeper toward a warmer chamber ahead.

locationHints.context: descending from the ice bridge and probing the first forked passages within the labyrinth
locationHints.notes: This beat covers movement from the entrance chamber deeper into Zeyfa's Labyrinth and ends just before the party reaches the warmer fissure chamber.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: She is terrified of the labyrinth but agrees to keep moving with the party.

itemDetails.name: Romil's token
itemDetails.notes: Kalima clutches the glowing token while praying to Romil for protection from ice and snow.

sourceEntries.title: The Booming Fissure
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: In the warm room the party studies the chasm-like split, tests the icy fern growth, triggers worsening wind and heat from below, and finally commits to crossing after the first failed leap.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party studies a warm fissure lined with ice ferns, watches the room grow more violent, and finally commits to crossing after Eolo's failed leap proves how dangerous it is.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The tunnels open into a broad chamber split by a dark crack with warm air rising from below and strange ice ferns waving at the edge. As the party experiments with light, distance, and possible routes, the wind and snow intensify around the fissure, Kalima grows more alarmed, and Eolo's slipping attempt shows that the crossing is riskier than it first looked; by the end of the beat, the group decides they have to get everyone across anyway.

locationHints.context: warm fissure chamber, a wide room split by a dark crack with ice ferns and warm air below
locationHints.notes: This fixed location is a room within Zeyfa's Labyrinth, and the party remains here throughout the beat while testing the hazard and deciding how to cross.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: She reacts fearfully as the chamber grows more dangerous and wants the party to turn back.

sourceEntries.title: Past the Storm Gate
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After organizing the crossing, the group forces everyone through the booming barrier, adapts to the shifting passages beyond it, and presses forward until a frozen side chamber appears.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party forces a crossing over the fissure and pushes onward through storm-lashed corridors where the maze shifts and ice grows beneath every step.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Using flight, levitation, rope, and brute force, the party gets everyone across the fissure, with Justas briefly plunging into the gap and discovering a pocket of hot, lightless blackness inside it. Beyond the crossing, the labyrinth becomes harsher rather than safer: the wind howls, exits move, visibility drops, and ice thickens underfoot as the group follows changing passages deeper toward a side chamber ahead.

locationHints.context: crossing the crack and navigating shifting icy corridors farther into the labyrinth
locationHints.notes: Inside the fissure the air feels hot and vision cuts out, but the beat mostly covers dangerous travel through deeper parts of Zeyfa's Labyrinth on the far side.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: The party has to coordinate magic and flight to get her safely over the fissure.

sourceEntries.title: Questions for the Dead
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party discovers a frozen corpse, uses Speak with Dead to learn about Melusa, Zeyfa's sacrifice cycle, and the trapped soul, then gathers gear and relics before moving on.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: In a frozen corpse chamber, the party questions a dead villager about Melusa and Zeyfa's sacrifices, then loots a few useful relics before moving on.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party discovers a human corpse pinned to the wall by ice and uses Speak with Dead to learn that he came from Melusa, died when the wind came alive, and had entered the labyrinth as a sunrise sacrifice. Kalima confirms the village name, the corpse describes Melusa as a cliffside settlement fed by a river and fish, and Finnan's ancestral memory links the name to Melua, an old waterfall Kestavo on the Teft; once the questioning ends, the party searches the body and takes a few salvageable items before continuing down the passage.

locationHints.context: corpse chamber, a small frozen side chamber with a corpse pinned to the wall by ice
locationHints.notes: This fixed location is a chamber within Zeyfa's Labyrinth; the passage continues beyond it, but the beat centers on the questioning and search.

npcDetails.name: Unnamed man of Melusa
npcDetails.notes: He says the village chose him at sunrise, the wind killed him, and his soul still feels trapped in the maze.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: She recognizes Melusa immediately and adds what she can about the village and its lost gods.

npcDetails.name: Zeyfa
npcDetails.context: cruel west wind tied to sacrifice and the labyrinth
npcDetails.notes: The corpse identifies the wind's killer presence as Zeyfa's minion and frames the maze as Zeyfa's domain.

npcDetails.name: Melua
npcDetails.context: waterfall Kestavo recalled from halfling memory
npcDetails.notes: Finnan connects Melusa's name to an old shrine on the Teft associated with Melua.

itemDetails.name: plain silver ring
itemDetails.notes: A simple silver ring worn on a necklace by the corpse, taken as the most distinctive token from his body.

organizationDetails.name: Kestavo
organizationDetails.notes: The discussion of Zeyfa, Melua, sacrifice, and the dead man's trapped soul all turns on kestavo belief.

sourceEntries.title: The Looping White Maze
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party leaves the corpse behind, follows booming corridors into impossible loops, glimpses wind-formed hunters, and finally sneaks through the collapsing maze into calmer passage.

locationHints.notes: The same room repeats with changing exits until the party narrows this section of Zeyfa's Labyrinth down to a single path out.

npcDetails.name: Kalima
npcDetails.notes: She is hurt when the wind buffets the kenku carrying her and becomes even more fearful afterward.

npcDetails.name: wind-creatures
npcDetails.context: shadowy hunters sweeping the blizzard rooms
npcDetails.notes: They appear as bone-and-wind humanoid forms and force the party to retreat, loop, and finally sneak past.
